---
title: "Publications"
---

::: {.pub-item}
[Variational Approximations for Robust Bayesian Inference via Rho-Posteriors](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.07325){.pub-title}

[El Mahdi Khribch, Pierre Alquier]{.pub-authors}

[*Preprint*]{.pub-venue}

We study variational approximations of $\rho$-posteriors, which replace the log-likelihood with a general loss $\rho$, yielding robustness to model misspecification. We derive PAC-Bayesian bounds of the form:
$$\mathbb{E}_{\hat{\rho}}[\ell(\theta)] \leq \inf_{\rho \in \mathcal{P}} \left\{ \mathbb{E}_{\rho}[\ell(\theta)] + \frac{1}{\lambda}\mathrm{KL}(\rho \| \pi) \right\} + \varepsilon_n$$
:::

::: {.pub-item}
[On importance sampling and independent Metropolis--Hastings with an unbounded weight function](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.09514){.pub-title}

[George Deligiannidis, Pierre E. Jacob, El Mahdi Khribch, Guanyang Wang]{.pub-authors}

[*Major revision at The Annals of Statistics*]{.pub-venue}

We establish convergence conditions for importance sampling and independent Metropolis--Hastings when the weight function $w(\theta) = \pi(\theta)/q(\theta)$ is unbounded. We show that finite variance $\mathrm{Var}_q[w(\theta)] < \infty$ is sufficient but not necessary, and provide sharp conditions on the tail behaviour of $w$.
:::

::: {.pub-item}
[Convergence of Statistical Estimators via Mutual Information Bounds](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.18539){.pub-title}

[El Mahdi Khribch, Pierre Alquier]{.pub-authors}

[*Submitted and under review for Journal of Machine Learning Research*]{.pub-venue}

We introduce a general framework connecting mutual information to the convergence of statistical estimators. For a posterior $\rho$ over parameters $\theta$ given data $Y_{1:n}$, we derive bounds of the form:
$$\mathbb{E}[R(\hat{\theta}_n)] \leq \frac{I(\Theta; Y_{1:n}) + \log(1/\delta)}{n}$$
with applications to PAC-Bayes, aggregation, and online learning.
:::
